_source: Bound-level direct summation over the complete ASD59 energy-level set in libs_production.db (U(T) = sum_i g_i exp(-E_i/kT) for E_i < IP).
_description: Reference partition function values U(T) at LIBS-relevant temperatures
_note: Regenerated 2026-06 from the complete ASD59 NIST/Kurucz energy levels. Convention: sum bound levels only (E_i < ionization potential), no IPD, matching SahaBoltzmannSolver.calculate_partition_function with n_e=None. Cross-validated against the gold-standard Barklem & Collet 2016 (A&A 588, A96) atomic partition functions (their table8_vNov2022, computed from complete NIST level lists): 212 species-temp comparisons at 5000/10000 K agree to median 0.017%, mean 0.7%; all LIBS test elements (Fe,Cu,Al,Ni,Ti,Cr) within ~1.2%. The prior values were a frozen snapshot of an INCOMPLETE pre-M5 DB (e.g. Cr II 10000K read 13.21 vs the correct 18.48 = B&C 18.48), not a pre-cleanup higher reference. 15000/20000 K extend the same B&C-validated direct sum beyond B&C's 10000 K ceiling.
